# Codebase analysis for website modifications (2026-02-24 18:10Z)

_**User**_

analyze the codebase totally we will now start modifying the website

---

_**Agent (model claude-4.5-opus-high-thinking, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user wants me to analyze the codebase completely before they start modifying the website. This is a good use case for the explore subagent to get a comprehensive understanding of the codebase structure, technologies used, and key components.

Let me launch an exploration task to understand the codebase thoroughly.</details></think>

---

I'll analyze the codebase thoroughly to understand its structure, technologies, and components before we start making modifications.

---

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="task_v2">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **task_v2**</summary>


Parameters:

```json
{
  "description": "Analyze bakery website codebase",
  "prompt": "Perform a very thorough analysis of this bakery website codebase. I need to understand:\n\n1. **Project Structure**: What folders and files exist? How is the project organized?\n2. **Technology Stack**: What framework, libraries, and dependencies are being used? Check package.json and any config files.\n3. **Components**: What React/UI components exist? List all components and their purposes.\n4. **Styling**: How is styling handled? (CSS, Tailwind, styled-components, etc.)\n5. **Pages/Routes**: What pages or routes exist in the application?\n6. **Assets**: What images, fonts, or other assets are included?\n7. **Configuration**: Any special configuration files (vite, webpack, tsconfig, etc.)?\n8. **State Management**: How is state managed if applicable?\n9. **Key Features**: What main features does this bakery website have based on the code?\n\nProvide a comprehensive summary with file paths and descriptions of each major part of the codebase.",
  "subagentType": "explore",
  "model": "composer-1.5",
  "name": "explore"
}
```

ThemeContext = createContext&lt;ThemeContextType | undefined&gt;(undefined);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nexport function ThemeProvider({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) {\\\\\\\\n  const [theme, Technology Stack\\\\n\\\\n| Category | Technology |\\\\n|----------|------------|\\\\n| **Framework** | Next.js 14.2 (App Router) |\\\\n| **UI** | React 18.3 |\\\\n| **Language** | TypeScript 5.x |\\\\n| **Styling** | Tailwind CSS 3.4 + PostCSS + Autoprefixer |\\\\n| **Fonts** | Google Fonts (Inter, Playfair Display) via &#96;next/font&#96; |\\\\n| **Linting** | ESLint with &#96;eslint-config-next&#96; |\\\\n\\\\n**Dependencies** (&#96;package.json&#96;):\\\\n\\\\n- &#96;next&#96;, &#96;react&#96;, &#96;react-dom&#96;\\\\n- Dev: &#96;@types/node&#96;, &#96;@types/react&#96;, &#96;@types/react-dom&#96;, &#96;autoprefixer&#96;, &#96;eslint&#96;, &#96;eslint-config-next&#96;, &#96;postcss&#96;, &#96;tailwindcss&#96;, &#96;typescript&#96;\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 3. Components\\\\n\\\\n| Component | Path | Purpose |\\\\n|-----------|------|---------|\\\\n| **Navigation** | &#96;src/components/Navigation.tsx&#96; | Fixed nav with logo, links, theme toggle, reduced-motion toggle, mobile menu |\\\\n| **HeroSection** | &#96;src/components/HeroSection.tsx&#96; | Hero with scroll-driven frame animation (285 frames), loading bar, CTAs |\\\\n| **ScrollStorySection** | &#96;src/components/ScrollStorySection.tsx&#96; | “Our Story” section with 4 process steps |\\\\n| **ProductsSection** | &#96;src/components/ProductsSection.tsx&#96; | Featured products grid |\\\\n| **ProductCard** | &#96;src/components/ProductCard.tsx&#96; | Product card with image, description, Shopify link, lazy load, skeleton |\\\\n| **BrandPromiseSection** | &#96;src/components/BrandPromiseSection.tsx&#96; | Three brand promises (Handcrafted, Fresh Ingredients, Baked With Love) |\\\\n| **CTASection** | &#96;src/components/CTASection.tsx&#96; | Call-to-action with Shop Now, Instagram, WhatsApp |\\\\n| **Footer** | &#96;src/components/Footer.tsx&#96; | Footer with brand, shop links, social links, contact |\\\\n| **ThemeProvider** | &#96;src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx&#96; | Light/dark theme context and persistence |\\\\n| **ReducedMotionProvider** | &#96;src/components/ReducedMotionProvider.tsx&#96; | Reduced-motion preference context |\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 4. Styling\\\\n\\\\n- **Tailwind CSS** for utility classes and custom theme in &#96;tailwind.config.ts&#96;.\\\\n- **Custom theme**:\\\\n  - Colors: cream, chocolate, gold, beige\\\\n  - Fonts: Playfair Display (serif), Inter (sans)\\\\n  - Animations: fade-in, fade-up, scale-in, slide-in, pulse-soft, float\\\\n  - Shadows: soft, soft-lg, glow\\\\n- **&#96;globals.css&#96;**:\\\\n  - Tailwind base/components/utilities\\\\n  - CSS variables for colors and typography\\\\n  - Dark mode via &#96;.dark&#96;\\\\n  - &#96;prefers-reduced-motion&#96; support\\\\n  - Utility classes: &#96;.btn-primary&#96;, &#96;.btn-secondary&#96;, &#96;.btn-gold&#96;, &#96;.glass&#96;, &#96;.card-hover&#96;, &#96;.section&#96;, &#96;.heading-xl&#96;, etc.\\\\n  - Scroll animation classes: &#96;.animate-on-scroll&#96;, &#96;.animate-on-scroll-left&#96;, etc.\\\\n  - Skeleton loader styles\\\\n- **Google Fonts** imported in &#96;globals.css&#96; and used via &#96;next/font&#96; in layout.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 5. Pages / Routes\\\\n\\\\nSingle-page app with one route:\\\\n\\\\n| Route | File | Content |\\\\n|-------|------|---------|\\\\n| &#96;/&#96; | &#96;src/app/page.tsx&#96; | Home page with all sections |\\\\n\\\\nSections are anchored for in-page navigation:\\\\n\\\\n- &#96;#hero&#96; – Hero\\\\n- &#96;#story&#96; – Our Story\\\\n- &#96;#products&#96; – Products\\\\n- &#96;#cta&#96; – Call to action\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 6. Assets\\\\n\\\\n| Type | Location | Files |\\\\n|------|----------|-------|\\\\n| **Product images** | &#96;public/assets/images/&#96; | &#96;cupcakes.svg&#96;, &#96;cookies.svg&#96;, &#96;kunafa.svg&#96; |\\\\n| **Hero animation** | &#96;public/assets/animations/&#96; | Expected: &#96;ezgif-frame-001.jpg&#96; … &#96;ezgif-frame-285.jpg&#96; (not present in backup) |\\\\n| **Fonts** | Google Fonts | Inter, Playfair Display |\\\\n| **OG image** | Referenced in metadata | &#96;/og-image.jpg&#96; (not present) |\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 7. Configuration\\\\n\\\\n| File | Purpose |\\\\n|------|---------|\\\\n| **next.config.js** | &#96;output: 'export'&#96; for static export, &#96;images.unoptimized: true&#96;, &#96;optimizeCss&#96;, &#96;removeConsole&#96; in production |\\\\n| **tailwind.config.ts** | Content paths, dark mode &#96;class&#96;, custom theme (colors, fonts, animations, shadows) |\\\\n| **postcss.config.js** | Tailwind and Autoprefixer |\\\\n| **tsconfig.json** | Path alias &#96;@/*&#96; → &#96;./src/*&#96;, Next.js plugin |\\\\n| **.eslintrc.json** | &#96;next/core-web-vitals&#96;, &#96;react/no-unescaped-entities&#96; off, &#96;@next/next/no-img-element&#96; off |\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 8. State Management\\\\n\\\\nNo Redux/Zustand/etc. State is handled with:\\\\n\\\\n- **React Context**:\\\\n  - &#96;ThemeProvider&#96; – theme (light/dark) and &#96;localStorage&#96;\\\\n  - &#96;ReducedMotionProvider&#96; – reduced-motion preference and &#96;localStorage&#96;\\\\n- **Local component state** – &#96;useState&#96; for UI (e.g. menu open, scroll state).\\\\n- **Custom hooks** – &#96;useIntersectionObserver&#96; for scroll-based visibility.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 9. Main Features\\\\n\\\\n1. **Scroll-driven hero animation** – 285-frame sequence driven by scroll; optional reduced-motion mode.\\\\n2. **Product showcase** – Cupcakes, cookies, kunafa chocolate bars with links to Shopify.\\\\n3. **Brand story** – Four-step process (oven, crafting, baking, ready).\\\\n4. **Brand promises** – Handcrafted, fresh ingredients, baked with love.\\\\n5. **Theme switching** – Light/dark with &#96;localStorage&#96; persistence.\\\\n6. **Reduced motion** – Toggle and &#96;prefers-reduced-motion&#96; support.\\\\n7. **Responsive layout** – Mobile menu and responsive grid.\\\\n8. **Analytics hooks** – &#96;trackProductClick&#96; and &#96;trackCTAClick&#96; in &#96;shopify.ts&#96; (ready for gtag).\\\\n9. **Social links** – Instagram, Facebook, Twitter, WhatsApp.\\\\n10. **Static export** – Configured for static hosting via &#96;output: 'export'&#96;.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Summary\\\\n\\\\nThe project is a Next.js 14 single-page bakery site with Tailwind CSS, scroll-driven hero animation, product showcase, and Shopify integration. It uses React Context for theme and reduced-motion preferences, and is set up for static export. The hero animation expects 285 JPG frames in &#96;public/assets/animations/&#96;; those files are not present in the current ## Bakery Website Codebase Analysis

Here's a complete overview of your bakery website:

### Technology Stack
| Category | Technology |
|----------|------------|
| Framework | **Next.js 14.2** (App Router) |
| UI | **React 18.3** |
| Language | **TypeScript 5.x** |
| Styling | **Tailwind CSS 3.4** |
| Fonts | Google Fonts (Inter, Playfair Display) |

### Project Structure
```
bakery-showcase/
├── src/
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── layout.tsx        # Root layout with metadata
│   │   ├── page.tsx          # Home page (single-page app)
│   │   └── globals.css       # Global styles & CSS variables
│   ├── components/           # 10 React components
│   ├── hooks/                # Custom hooks
│   └── lib/                  # Constants & Shopify integration
├── public/assets/            # Images (SVG product icons)
└── config files              # next.config, tailwind.config, etc.
```

### Components Overview
| Component | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `Navigation.tsx` | Fixed nav with logo, links, theme toggle, mobile menu |
| `HeroSection.tsx` | Hero with scroll-driven 285-frame animation |
| `ScrollStorySection.tsx` | "Our Story" with 4 process steps |
| `ProductsSection.tsx` | Featured products grid |
| `ProductCard.tsx` | Product card with Shopify link |
| `BrandPromiseSection.tsx` | Three brand promises section |
| `CTASection.tsx` | Call-to-action (Shop, Instagram, WhatsApp) |
| `Footer.tsx` | Footer with links & contact |
| `ThemeProvider.tsx` | Light/dark theme context |
| `ReducedMotionProvider.tsx` | Accessibility motion preference |

### Key Features
1. **Scroll-driven hero animation** - 285-frame sequence controlled by scroll
2. **Product showcase** - Cupcakes, cookies, kunafa with Shopify links
3. **Brand story** - Four-step baking process visualization
4. **Theme switching** - Light/dark mode with localStorage persistence
5. **Reduced motion support** - Accessibility toggle
6. **Responsive design** - Mobile menu and responsive grids
7. **Analytics hooks** - Ready for Google Analytics integration
8. **Static export** - Configured for static hosting

### Custom Styling
- **Color palette**: cream, chocolate, gold, beige
- **Custom animations**: fade-in, fade-up, scale-in, slide-in, pulse-soft, float
- **Utility classes**: `.btn-primary`, `.btn-gold`, `.glass`, `.card-hover`

### Notes
- **Missing assets**: The hero animation expects 285 JPG frames in `public/assets/animations/` that aren't present in this backup
- **Missing OG image**: `/og-image.jpg` referenced in metadata isn't present
- **Single route**: This is a single-page application with anchor navigation (#hero, #story, #products, #cta)

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The SVG files are valid and contain actual graphics. They should be displaying. 

Looking at the screenshot more carefully, I see the problem - the images load but then seem to disappear or show as empty brown areas. The issue might be:

1. The `loading="lazy"` attribute combined with `opacity-0` when not loaded - the image starts with opacity-0 and only becomes visible when `imageLoaded` is true
2. The intersection observer might be causing issues
3. The image might load before the component has fully mounted and the `onLoad` event is missed

One common issue is that when using `loading="lazy"`, the `onLoad` event might fire before React attaches the event handler, especially on fast connections or cached images. This means `imageLoaded` never becomes `true` and the image stays at `opacity-0`.

Let me fix this by:
1. Adding a check for `img.complete` when the component mounts
2. Or removing the lazy loading
3. Or adjusting the opacity logic

The most robust fix is to check if the image is already loaded when the component mounts.</details></think>

---

I found the issue! The SVG files are valid, but there's a timing problem with the image loading. When images load quickly (like cached SVGs), the `onLoad` event can fire before React attaches the handler, leaving `imageLoaded` as `false` and the image stuck at `opacity-0`.

Let me fix the `ProductCard.tsx`:

---

Fixed! The issue was a race condition with image loading. Here's what I changed in `ProductCard.tsx`:

### Problem
When images load very quickly (especially cached SVGs), the browser's `onLoad` event fires before React attaches the event handler. This left `imageLoaded` as `false`, keeping the image at `opacity-0` (invisible).

### Solution
1. **Added a ref to the image** - to check its loading state directly
2. **Added useEffect check** - on component mount, checks if image is already loaded (`img.complete`)
3. **Removed `loading="lazy"`** - this was causing timing issues with the load event

```tsx
const imgRef = useRef<HTMLImageElement>(null);

useEffect(() => {
  if (imgRef.current?.complete) {
    setImageLoaded(true);
  }
}, []);
```

Refresh your page and the product images should now display correctly!

Done! Here's what I improved for the "Order Now" button:

### Changes Made

**Desktop (md and up):**
- Gold background (`bg-gold`) with dark text for high visibility
- Responsive padding: `py-2.5 px-5` on medium screens, `py-3 px-6` on large screens
- Added shadow for depth (`shadow-md`)
- Hover effects: lighter gold, shadow, and slight lift
- Rounded pill shape (`rounded-full`)

**Mobile:**
- Added a compact "Order" button next to the hamburger menu
- Smaller size (`py-2 px-4`) but still visible
- Same gold styling for consistency
- Always visible without opening the menu

### Button Styling Summary
| Screen | Button Text | Styling |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| Mobile | "Order" | Compact gold button, always visible |
| Desktop | "Order Now" | Full gold button with hover effects |

The button now:
- Stands out with gold color on any background
- Has proper shadow for depth
- Is responsive across all screen sizes
- Is always visible on mobile (no need to open menu)

- **Polite** - "Kindly" is respectful without being overly formal

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is asking me to create a WhatsApp marketing message for their bakery. This is a content/copywriting request, not a code change. I can help with this by providing suggestions and examples.

Let me craft a professional WhatsApp marketing message for their bakery based on what I know about their products (Artisan Cupcakes, Gourmet Cookies, Kunafa Chocolate Bars).</details></think>

---

Here are some WhatsApp marketing message templates for your bakery:

---

### 1. New Customer Welcome Message
```
Assalam o Alaikum! 🌟

Welcome to *Premium Bakery*!

We're thrilled to have you here. Check out our delicious treats:

🧁 *Artisan Cupcakes* - Handcrafted perfection
🍪 *Gourmet Cookies* - Crispy outside, soft inside  
🍫 *Kunafa Chocolate Bars* - Middle Eastern fusion delight

📦 *Fresh baked daily & delivered to your doorstep!*

Reply with the product name to order!

JazakAllah for choosing us! 💛
```

---

### 2. Weekly Special / Promotion
```
Assalam o Alaikum! 🎉

*This Week's Special at Premium Bakery!*

🔥 Order 6 Cupcakes, Get 2 FREE!
🔥 Kunafa Chocolate Bars - Now Available!

✨ Freshly baked with love
✨ Premium ingredients only
✨ Fast home delivery

📞 Reply "ORDER" to place your order now!

Limited stock - Don't miss out! 💛
```

---

### 3. Festive / Eid Message
```
Assalam o Alaikum! 🌙✨

*Eid Mubarak from Premium Bakery!*

Make your celebration sweeter with our special treats:

🧁 Cupcake Box (12 pcs)
🍪 Cookie Gift Box
🍫 Kunafa Chocolate Bars

🎁 *Special Eid packaging available!*
🚚 *Pre-order now for guaranteed delivery*

Reply to place your order!

Eid Mubarak! 💛
```

---

### 4. Re-engagement Message (Inactive Customers)
```
Assalam o Alaikum! 👋

We miss you at *Premium Bakery*!

It's been a while since your last order. We've got some amazing new treats waiting for you!

🆕 Try our *Kunafa Chocolate Bars* - customers are loving it!

Reply "MENU" to see what's fresh today!

JazakAllah! 💛
```

---

### Tips for WhatsApp Marketing:
- **Keep it short** - People skim messages
- **Use bold** with `*text*` for emphasis
- **Add emojis** sparingly for visual appeal
- **Clear CTA** - Tell them exactly what to do (Reply, Order, etc.)
- **Timing** - Send between 10 AM - 8 PM
- **Frequency** - Don't spam, 1-2 messages per week max

Would you like me to customize any of these further? If you want me to add these as templates in your code, switch to Agent mode and let me know!

### To Add Your Logo:

Please copy your `logo-removebg-preview.png` file to:
```
D:\bakers website - BACKUP\bakery-showcase\public\assets\images\
```

And rename it to `logo.png`

**Or run this command** (replace the path to your logo file):
```powershell
copy "YOUR_LOGO_PATH\logo-removebg-preview.png" "D:\bakers website - BACKUP\bakery-showcase\public\assets\images\logo.png"
```

The logo will appear in:
- **Navigation bar** (header) - 40px height on mobile, 48px on desktop
- **Footer** - 48px height

Once you copy the logo file, refresh the page and it will show up!

Done! I've created a complete product modal system with pricing options. Here's the summary:

### New Product Modal Features:

**1. Product Details:**
- Large product image
- Full description
- Category badge

**2. Size/Price Options:**
| Product | Options |
|---------|---------|
| **Cupcakes** | Single (Rs.150), Box of 6 (Rs.800), Box of 12 (Rs.1,500), Party Pack 24 (Rs.2,800) |
| **Brownies** | Single (Rs.200), Box of 4 (Rs.700), Box of 8 (Rs.1,300), Full Tray 16 (Rs.2,400) |
| **Sundae** | Small (Rs.250), Medium (Rs.400), Large (Rs.550), Family (Rs.900) |
| **Chocolate Chip Butter Cake** | Slice (Rs.300), Half 500g (Rs.1,200), Full 1kg (Rs.2,200), Large 2kg (Rs.4,000) |
| **Pizza** | Small 6" (Rs.500), Medium 9" (Rs.900), Large 12" (Rs.1,300), Family 15" (Rs.1,800) |
| **Chicken Bread** | Single (Rs.180), Pack of 3 (Rs.500), Pack of 6 (Rs.950), Dozen (Rs.1,800) |

**3. Order Form:**
- Size/option selector (click to select)
- Quantity selector (+/-)
- Delivery address input
- Total price calculation

**4. WhatsApp Order:**
- Green "Order on WhatsApp" button
- Pre-filled message with all order details:
  ```
  Assalam o Alaikum! *I'd love to order from Nabia's Eatry!*
  
  *Order Details:*
  Product: Pizza
  Size/Option: Large (12")
  Quantity: 2
  Total: Rs. 2,600
  
  My Address: [Customer's address]
  
  JazakAllah!
  ```

**5. Product Cards Updated:**
- Shows "From Rs. X" price badge
- Click anywhere to open modal
- "View Options" on hover
- "Order Now" button

Refresh your page to see the new product modal system!

Done! The modal is now super responsive. Here's what I fixed:

### Responsive Improvements:

**Mobile (Bottom Sheet Style):**
- Opens from the bottom like a native app
- Takes 92% of screen height
- Rounded top corners only
- Sticky order button at bottom
- Slide-up animation
- No navbar overlap

**Desktop/Tablet:**
- Centered modal card
- Max width: 672px
- Scale-in animation
- Standard rounded corners

### Key Features:
| Feature | Mobile | Desktop |
|---------|--------|---------|
| Position | Bottom sheet | Centered |
| Height | 92vh | Auto (max 90vh) |
| Animation | Slide up | Scale in |
| Order Button | Full width, sticky | Side-by-side with total |
| Image | 176px | 240px |
| Close button | Top right, always visible | Same |

### Layout:
- **Image** - Responsive height with gradient overlay
- **Content** - Scrollable with proper spacing
- **Footer** - Sticky with total + order button
- **Z-index 100** - Above navbar (which is z-50)

Done! Fixed the responsiveness:

### Changes Made:

**Breakpoint Changes:**
- Desktop nav now shows at `lg` (1024px) instead of `md` (768px)
- Mobile/tablet view extends up to 1024px

**Button Sizing:**
| Screen Size | Button Style |
|-------------|--------------|
| Mobile (<640px) | `py-1.5 px-3` text `10px` |
| Tablet (640-1024px) | `py-2 px-4` text `12px` |
| Desktop (>1024px) | `py-2.5 px-5` text `14px` |

**Other Fixes:**
- Added `whitespace-nowrap` to prevent text wrapping
- Reduced gaps for better spacing
- Changed mobile button text from "Order" to "Order Now"

Refresh your page - the button should now scale properly at all screen sizes!

---

Done! Here's the updated order message with essential business fields:

### Updated Message Format:

```
Assalam o Alaikum! *I'd love to order from Nabia's Eatry!*

*Order Details:*
Product: Cupcakes
Size/Option: Box of 6
Quantity: 2
Total: Rs. 1,600

*Delivery Info:*
Name: 
Phone: 
Address: 
Preferred Time: 

Payment: Cash on Delivery
Notes: 

JazakAllah!
```

---

### Essential Fields for Food Business:

| Field | Why It's Important |
|-------|-------------------|
| **Name** | Know who the order is for |
| **Phone** | Contact for delivery/confirmation |
| **Address** | Delivery location |
| **Preferred Time** | When they want it delivered |
| **Payment** | Payment method (COD) |
| **Notes** | Special requests, allergies, instructions (e.g., "No nuts", "Extra spicy", "Ring doorbell") |

### Other Suggestions You Could Add Later:

1. **Delivery Charges** - Show "Free delivery over Rs. X" or "Delivery: Rs. 100"
2. **Minimum Order** - "Minimum order Rs. 500"
3. **Operating Hours** - Show delivery timings on website
4. **Estimated Delivery Time** - "Delivery in 30-45 mins"
5. **WhatsApp Business** - Use WhatsApp Business for auto-replies & catalog

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I see the structure. The page is a Server Component (no 'use client' directive). The modal state is managed in ProductsSection.

2. Use a different approach like a custom event or context

The cleanest solution would be to:
1. Create a shared context for the modal state
2. Or use a simpler approach - have the hero buttons scroll to products and trigger the modal

Actually, a simpler approach would be to:
1. Create a global state management using a simple event system
2. Or better yet, make the page a client component and lift the state up

Let me think about the simplest approach...

Actually, I'll use a different strategy - I'll make the hero buttons programmatically trigger a click on a hidden element or use a custom event system. But that's complex.

The cleanest solution for this small project is:
1. Create a context for product modal
2. Wrap the app with it
3. Both HeroSection and ProductsSection can use it

Let me create a ProductModalContext.</details></think>

---

All done. Now when you click on "Order Cupcakes", "Order Brownies", or "Order Pizza" buttons in the hero section, the product modal will open directly with that product's details.

Here's what I changed:

1. **Created `ProductModalContext.tsx`** - A shared context to manage modal state across components

2. **Updated `layout.tsx`** - Wrapped the app with `ProductModalProvider` so the modal state is accessible everywhere

3. **Updated `HeroSection.tsx`** - Changed the CTA buttons from anchor links to buttons that call `openModal()` with the corresponding product

4. **Updated `ProductsSection.tsx`** - Now uses the shared context instead of local state, so both components share the same modal
